page.content: In the analysis of serious adverse events, there were 13 serious adverse events (SAE) in 12/31 patients, including 1 case of necrotizing leukoencephalopathy causing therapy discontinuation, 1 case of radiogenic gliosis, and 7 cases of sepsis (2 Ommaya infection and 1 post-op). Additionally, there was 1 case of recurrent infections causing therapy discontinuation, 1 case of coxitis with candida, 1 case of pneumonia causing discontinuation of maintenance, and 1 case of shunt insufficiency causing death not due to disease. Regarding the cost analysis, for the INTEGRA group (n = 6), the mean hospital stay cost was $9,572.5 (3030.8) with a median of $8,205.0 (7658.0–13,128.0), while for the Flap group (n = 5), it was $17,066.4 (9939.8) with a median of $19,692.0 (8205.0–21,333.0).

cells.cell_text: Serious adverse events (13 SAE in 12/31 patients)
Shunt insufficiency causing death (not due to disease)

page.content: Surgery costs for the INTEGRA group were $3,066.2 (583.4) with a median of $2,880.1 (2693.9–3418.6), and for the Flap group, they were $6,305.0 (4174.5) with a median of $5,609.3 (3113.4–9143.4). Outpatient costs for the INTEGRA group were $287.8 (305.8) with a median of $184.3 (124.1–222.3), while for the Flap group, they were $353.4 (108.4) with a median of $389.62 (231.6–439.1). Total costs amounted to $12,926.5 (3210.9) for the INTEGRA group with a median of $11,825.0 (10,694.9–15,751.1), and for the Flap group, it was $23,583.5 (12,675.0) with a median of $23,244.5 (17,348.4–26,942.3).
